title: Ampelocissus compositifolia
intro: Ampelocissus compositifolia is a plant species commonly known as the 'Dhundal' or 'Himalayan Grape', which is a type of climbing vine belonging to the Vitaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, exhibiting health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ampelocissus compositifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared in the form of decoctions, infusions, and ointments, and is used to treat various health conditions, with its leaves and fruits being commonly used in herbal preparations.
taxonomy: Ampelocissus compositifolia is classified within the family Vitaceae. This species belongs to the genus Ampelocissus. The classification of Ampelocissus compositifolia is further defined as a species of flowering plant. It is native to Asia and is commonly found in the tropical regions of the continent. The specific classification of Ampelocissus compositifolia provides insight into its evolutionary history and taxonomic relationships.
